## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On September 7, 1940, the incredibly massive, absolute, and deeply horrific aerial bombardment campaign known as "The Blitz" aggressively commenced during World War II. The massive German Luftwaffe aggressively unleashed a terrifying, relentless bombing campaign against London and other major British cities. For exactly 57 consecutive nights, massive waves of German bombers aggressively targeted industrial centers and civilian populations, resulting in horrific, massive casualties. The incredible, unbreakable resilience of the British public during this massive terror campaign absolutely secured their ultimate national survival.

On September 7, 1822, the incredibly massive, historically definitive, and fiercely aggressive independence of Brazil was officially declared. Prince Dom Pedro, the heir to the Portuguese throne, aggressively drew his sword on the banks of the Ipiranga River and famously shouted the massive, iconic "Cry of Ipiranga," declaring "Independence or Death!" This massive, bold severance from the Portuguese Empire fundamentally birthed the Empire of Brazil, aggressively establishing the absolute largest, massive sovereign nation in South America without the horrific bloodshed that characterized Spanish colonial revolutions.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On September 7, 1906, the incredibly massive, highly foundational, and absolutely historic Bank of India was officially and aggressively founded in Bombay (Mumbai). Established by a massive, highly influential group of prominent Indian businessmen led by Sassoon J. David, this massive financial institution aggressively aimed to serve the massive indigenous merchant class that was heavily ignored by British colonial banks. It fundamentally became an absolute, massive pillar of the Indian banking sector, heavily fueling the massive commercial expansion of the subcontinent.

On September 7, 1965, the incredibly massive, fiercely brutal, and absolute aerial warfare of the Indo-Pakistani War heavily escalated. Following the massive Indian Army advance toward Lahore, the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) aggressively launched a massive, highly coordinated preemptive strike against major Indian Air Force bases, notably hitting Pathankot, Adampur, and Halwara. This massive, aggressive aerial bombardment fundamentally escalated the conflict into an absolute, total war, drawing the incredibly fierce, massive retaliation of the Indian Air Force across the entire massive western theater.
